question: Is the image divided into two equal-width panels (left and right) separated by a thin gray vertical line?
Does the left panel depict a planar 2R robotic arm schematic with a base frame at origin O (black arrows for x-right, y-up)?
Is the first link (O to J) in the left panel annotated with 'L1=3' using a light gray dimension line?
Is the second link (J to P) in the left panel annotated with 'L2=2'?
Is the angle 'θ1=30°' annotated at the base with a gray arc and arrow (counterclockwise from +x)?
Is the relative angle 'θ2=60°' annotated at elbow J with a gray arc?
Are joints O and J depicted as black solid circles, and end-effector P as a blue solid circle labeled 'P' in the left panel?
Are there thin gray dashed lines projecting P onto the x and y axes in the left panel?
Is the coordinate text 'x≈2.60, y≈3.50' written near the projections in the left panel?
Does the right panel display black x–y coordinate axes and two concentric circles centered at origin O?
Is the outer circle visually approximately 5 times larger in radius than the inner circle?
Is the annular region between the circles filled with very light gray (no gradient) and labeled 'REACHABLE'?
Is point P in the right panel shown as a blue solid dot within the annular region (clearly outside inner circle and inside outer circle)?
Is the position of P in the right panel visually consistent with radius ~4.36 (closer to the outer circle boundary than the inner circle)?
Is point P in the right panel labeled 'P' with a thin gray leader line?
Is there a blue radius line drawn from the origin to P in the right panel, labeled 'r'?
Does the image strictly adhere to the color scheme (White background, Black/Gray lines/text, Blue point P) without shadows or gradients?
Are all text labels and numerical annotations clearly legible without blurring, overlapping, or non-Latin characters?
